memory.summary: With the afternoon free and the project barely touched, I'm torn between doing a quick chore to feel productive or just relaxing now. The easy win seems tempting, but my friends might be up for hanging out instead.
That tiny chore win feels good, but I'm already thinking about grabbing lunch with my friends instead of pushing harder on this project.
I took the easy win and feel good, but seeing the project still at the start makes my stomach churn. I just want to hang out with friends instead of dealing with this stress.

The relief of hanging out with friends feels real right now, but seeing that untouched project looming makes my stomach churn again. I'm trading future stress for present ease, hoping the guilt doesn't catch up later.

The friends' laughter feels like a warm blanket right now, completely drowning out the gnawing worry about that untouched project. I'm just glad the afternoon is finally done and we can keep hanging out.

desires: Keep the group vibe light and avoid any awkward confrontation about the unfinished term project.

goals.description: Sprint through the final 2 minutes of this trivial chore to silence the guilt before 22:00, so I can immediately jump back into the laugh-circle without looking like I'm rushing.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means I literally cannot see past this hour. The '3-day deadline' is abstract noise compared to the immediate social anxiety of finishing late. If I drop this now, the nagging guilt will ruin my vibe with cohort_3 during our meal. It's a small price to pay for keeping the peace.

goals.description: Fully immerse in the conversation with cohort_3 and mentally file the project work under 'tomorrow,' assuming that if I panic then, it'll be less awkward than right now.
goals.rationale: Relationships (0.5) are my top value; being the buzzkill or creating tension is a nightmare. My avoidant nature makes me fear conflict, so preserving the social flow right now is more important than pushing back on the looming deadline. I'll deal with the consequences tomorrow when the pressure is fresher.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing this trivial chore stops the guilt nagging at my brain so I can fully relax with cohort_3 later. I'll just do a quick burst now to clear the mental clutter, then join the laugh-circle without feeling like a buzzkill.
